Emulin distribution NOTICE
==========================

This distribution bundles third-party software. Below are the components,
their origin, and their licenses.


1. Emulin (this software)
-------------------------
Copyright (C) 1998-2026  Kiyoka Nishiyama
License: GNU General Public License version 2 (see COPYING)
Source : https://github.com/kiyoka/emulin


2. Microsoft Build of OpenJDK (bundled JRE under jre/)
-----------------------------------------------------
Provider : Microsoft (Microsoft Build of OpenJDK)
Version  : OpenJDK 25 (see jre/release for the exact build)
License  : GNU General Public License version 2 ONLY,
           with the "Classpath Exception"
           and the "OpenJDK Assembly Exception"
Full text: jre/legal/java.base/LICENSE
           jre/legal/java.base/ASSEMBLY_EXCEPTION
           jre/legal/java.base/ADDITIONAL_LICENSE_INFO
Source   : https://github.com/microsoft/openjdk
           https://github.com/openjdk/jdk25u

Per the Classpath Exception, linking this JRE from independent modules
does not by itself cause those modules to fall under the GPL. The bundled
JRE is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. To obtain the
corresponding source code of the bundled JRE, follow the URLs above.

Third-party licenses included in jre/legal/<module>/ cover:
  AES test vectors, ASM, c-libutl, CLDR, ICU, Public Suffix List,
  SipHash, Unicode data, etc. (see each .md file)


3. BusyBox (bundled under rootfs/bin/busybox)
---------------------------------------------
Provider : The BusyBox project (https://busybox.net/)
License  : GNU General Public License version 2
Source   : https://busybox.net/downloads/

The bundled busybox is a Linux x86-64 ELF binary that runs INSIDE the
Emulin emulator (it does not run as a native host process).


4. JLine 3 (linked into emulin-*-all.jar)
-----------------------------------------
Provider : The JLine project (https://github.com/jline/jline3)
License  : BSD-3-Clause


5. Real Linux binaries (demo bundle only — rootfs/usr/bin/, rootfs/usr/lib/)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
The "emulin-demo-*" zip bundles a Debian-derived rootfs containing
GNU coreutils, bash, git, curl, openssl, openssh, vim, perl, python,
emacs, and supporting libraries. Each binary retains its upstream license
(typically GPLv2/v3, LGPL, MIT, BSD, Apache, OpenSSL, Vim, or PSF).

These binaries are Linux ELF files executed by the Emulin emulator, not
by the host OS.

For the COMPLETE per-package license text (including the original
upstream copyright notices), see the Debian-standard machine-readable
copyright files bundled under:

  rootfs/usr/share/doc/<package>/copyright

These follow the DEP-5 format
(https://www.debian.org/doc/packaging-manuals/copyright-format/1.0/)
and are reproduced verbatim from the upstream Debian source packages.

For a one-page inventory of bundled packages with versions, licenses,
and source URLs, see THIRD-PARTY-LICENSES.md in this distribution.


GPL/LGPL source-availability notice
===================================
Emulin itself is distributed under GPL v2. The corresponding source for
this release is available at:

  https://github.com/kiyoka/emulin

For the bundled third-party binaries derived from Debian packages
(under rootfs/), the complete corresponding source code is available
from the Debian archive at:

  https://deb.debian.org/debian/pool/main/

For any package <pkg>, the source can be obtained via:

  apt-get source <pkg>

on a Debian system, or by manually downloading the .dsc / .orig.tar /
.debian.tar files matching the binary .deb under the URLs above.

In compliance with GPL v2 §3(b) / v3 §6(b), the maintainer of this
distribution offers to provide, for at least three (3) years from the
date of this release, a complete machine-readable copy of the
corresponding source code for any GPL/LGPL package bundled under
rootfs/, upon request, at no charge beyond the cost of physical media
and shipping. Contact via the GitHub repository above for such requests.

Microsoft Build of OpenJDK (jre/) and BusyBox (rootfs/bin/busybox) sources
are available at the URLs noted in sections 2 and 3 above.
